questions: What command structure should the CLI walkthrough feature use?
What output format should the CLI produce for both terminal users and coding agents?
Which server endpoint should the CLI call to fetch walkthrough data?
How should the CLI display architectural labels and diff excerpts?

answers.answer: A single top-level command: `hlx walkthrough [--ticket <id>] [--run <id>] [--format json|text]`. No subcommands — walkthrough consumption is a single read-only action. Follows existing patterns: --ticket flag with HELIX_TICKET_ID env fallback (from comments/index.ts:11-19), --run defaults to 'latest'. Code organized as module directory (walkthrough/index.ts + show.ts) matching comments/inspect patterns.
evidence: src/index.ts: 3 commands with switch-based routing — adding 4th is trivial
src/comments/index.ts lines 11-19: resolveTicketId pattern with --ticket flag and HELIX_TICKET_ID env
Product spec: 'hlx walkthrough command: New top-level command that fetches and displays pre-computed walkthrough data'
Product spec defers interactive TUI: 'MVP outputs formatted text/JSON'

answers.answer: Dual format via --format flag. Default 'text' renders a high-density step-by-step walkthrough: summary header with areas touched, then each step with file:line, category badge, description, and diff excerpt (~20 lines). '--format json' outputs the full WalkthroughResult wrapped with ticket/run metadata for coding agent consumption. No ANSI escape codes in MVP for universal compatibility.
evidence: Product spec: 'Dual output format: --format text (default) for human-readable terminal output; --format json for structured data consumable by coding agents'
Product spec: 'Structured for agents: CLI output must be machine-readable (JSON) so coding agents can consume walkthroughs programmatically'
src/comments/list.ts: existing text output pattern for terminal display

answers.answer: The new GET /tickets/:ticketId/runs/:runId/walkthrough endpoint (designed in server tech-research). Reads pre-computed walkthroughData JSONB from the DB. Supports 'latest' run resolution. Uses attachInspectionAuth for CLI auth (same as comments endpoint). The CLI calls hxFetch(config, path, { basePath: '/api' }) following the comments pattern. The existing POST /walkthrough is rejected because it triggers expensive ~60s Claude API regeneration.
evidence: SandboxRun.walkthroughData: pre-computed JSONB available for 45.9% of completed runs
Existing POST /walkthrough triggers generateWalkthrough which calls Claude API with 60s timeout
src/comments/list.ts line 25: hxFetch(config, path, { basePath: '/api' }) — proven CLI API call pattern
routes/api.ts line 164: comments GET uses attachInspectionAuth — same middleware for walkthrough GET

answers.answer: Architectural labels use the same categorizeFile() file-path pattern matching as the client and server (merge-analysis-service.ts:71-126). Each step in text output shows its category (e.g., [API Surface], [Schema], [Tests]) next to the title. The summary header lists all unique categories touched. Diff excerpts are inline per step when fileDiffs data is available — the text format shows the first ~20 lines of each file's diff. JSON format includes full diffs for coding agent consumption. Categorization is implemented as a shared utility (~30 lines) in src/lib/categorize-file.ts.
evidence: merge-analysis-service.ts lines 71-126: categorizeFile() implements the file-path pattern matching
Product spec: 'Architectural impact labels: Each walkthrough step is labeled by the area it touches'
Server tech-research: fileDiffs stored as Record<string, string> on WalkthroughRepoTour

statement: The CLI needs a new `hlx walkthrough` command that fetches pre-computed walkthrough data via the new server GET endpoint and renders it as text (default, with architectural category labels and diff excerpts) or JSON (for coding agents like Claude Code). The command follows existing CLI patterns (--ticket flag, HELIX_TICKET_ID env, hxFetch, module directory). No new external dependencies. Text format is optimized for 2-minute scan to answer 'Did this change respect major arteries?' JSON format provides full walkthrough data for programmatic agent consumption, replacing the current generic 'Continue with Claude Code' clipboard command.
